edges.description: Failed induction means the metanephros and its collecting system never develop.
edges.source: Failed ureteric bud outgrowth and metanephric induction

edges.description: In bilateral agenesis, absent fetal urine output leads to oligohydramnios.
Pulmonary hypoplasia leads to fatal respiratory insufficiency at birth.

nodes.description: Because the ureteric bud and metanephric mesenchyme fail to form a kidney, the affected side has no functioning renal tissue and no ureter. In unilateral disease the contralateral kidney undergoes compensatory hypertrophy and hyperfiltration; in bilateral disease there is no functional renal mass.

evidence.reference_title: A Gene Implicated in Activation of Retinoic Acid Receptor Targets Is a Novel Renal Agenesis Gene in Humans.
evidence.snippet: Bilateral renal agenesis is almost invariably fatal at birth, and unilateral renal agenesis can lead to future health issues including end-stage renal disease.

nodes.description: Renal agenesis originates from failure of the reciprocal inductive interaction between the ureteric bud and the metanephric mesenchyme. GDNF secreted by the metanephric mesenchyme signals through the RET receptor tyrosine kinase on the ureteric bud to drive bud outgrowth and branching; loss of GDNF-RET signaling, or of upstream regulators such as PAX2, GREB1L, ITGA8, and FGF20, prevents bud outgrowth or invasion of the mesenchyme, so the metanephros never forms.
nodes.id: Failed ureteric bud outgrowth and metanephric induction

evidence.reference_title: Genes and proteins in renal development.
evidence.snippet: Once it has formed, the mesenchyme secretes GDNF; this induces the nearby wolffian duct to produce a ureteric bud which invades the metanephrogenic mesenchyme and begins to arborize.

evidence.reference_title: The cellular basis of kidney development.
evidence.snippet: Ureteric bud outgrowth and branching morphogenesis are controlled by the Ret/Gdnf pathway, which is subject to positive and negative regulation by a variety of factors.

evidence.reference_title: Genes and proteins in renal development.
evidence.snippet: this event depends on the prior action in the intermediate mesoderm of transcription factors such as Lim-1, Pax-2, Eya-1, and Foxc-1.

nodes.description: Fetal urine is the major source of amniotic fluid in the second half of gestation. In bilateral renal agenesis the absence of urine output produces severe oligohydramnios, which mechanically compresses the fetus. The resulting deformation cascade is the Potter sequence: pulmonary hypoplasia (the usual cause of neonatal death), Potter facies, and limb contractures.

evidence.reference_title: Contemporary Outcomes of Patients with Isolated Bilateral Renal Agenesis with and without Fetal Intervention.
evidence.snippet: Bilateral renal agenesis (BRA) is a lethal diagnosis, specifically meaning that natural survival beyond birth is not expected secondary to pulmonary hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Pulmonary hypoplasia from oligohydramnios in bilateral renal agenesis leaves insufficient functional lung tissue for gas exchange, causing respiratory failure that is the proximate cause of death in most affected neonates.

evidence.reference_title: Contemporary Outcomes of Patients with Isolated Bilateral Renal Agenesis with and without Fetal Intervention.
evidence.snippet: Fetal intervention via amnioinfusion may promote pulmonary survivorship after birth, but postnatal survival remains poor.
